brief.projectDescription: An immersive, hand-painted style promotional landing page for a featured animated film, designed to evoke nostalgia and wonder.

productScope.mvpDefinition: A high-impact visual landing page featuring a trailer, story synopsis, character gallery, and ticket booking integration.
productScope.coreOutcome: Users feel emotionally connected to the film's aesthetic and proceed to book tickets or follow the project.
productScope.businessGoal: Drive ticket sales and social media engagement through high-quality visual storytelling.
productScope.audienceSummary: Animation fans, families, and Ghibli enthusiasts who value hand-drawn aesthetics and emotional narratives.
inScope: Immersive hero section with video background

roles.summary: A fan of animation looking for a new cinematic experience.

taskLoops.name: Discovery to Conversion
taskLoops.summary: The journey from landing on the page to deciding to see the movie.
taskLoops.entryTrigger: User clicks a social media ad or search result
steps: Read about the world and characters
Check local showtimes or streaming availability
taskLoops.successState: User clicks 'Get Tickets' or joins the mailing list

capabilities.name: Immersive Visual Experience
capabilities.summary: Using parallax and watercolor aesthetics to mimic the Ghibli art style.

capabilities.name: Ticketing & Lead Gen
capabilities.summary: Direct links to cinema partners and a newsletter for updates.

designIntent.colorDirection: Soft watercolor palette: lush forest greens, sky blues, warm sunset oranges, and creamy off-whites.
designIntent.style: Hand-drawn texture with soft edges, high-detail backgrounds, and fluid parallax scrolling.

informationArchitecture.navigationModel: Single-page immersive scroll with deep-link sub-pages for lore.

pageMap.title: The Journey Begins
pageMap.purpose: Primary landing experience to hook the audience.

pageMap.title: The World & Lore
pageMap.purpose: Detailed background on the film's setting and art direction.

layoutSections.contentHints: Logo (stylized), Story, Characters, Gallery, 'Get Tickets' CTA button with a glow effect.

designPlan.goal: Provide ethereal, non-distracting navigation that feels like part of the film's world.
designPlan.roleFit: Allows the visitor to jump to conversion or lore without breaking the visual spell.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Enables the 'Check local showtimes' step via the persistent CTA.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Reinforces visual storytelling through organic shapes and soft transitions.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Horizontal links with a prominent, stylized 'Get Tickets' button.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Floating, semi-transparent bar with rounded, organic edges.
designPlan.visualIntent: Glassmorphism with a watercolor tint; typography should be elegant and airy.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Soft hover glows and smooth scroll anchoring.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Minimalist links; prioritize the 'Get Tickets' action.
constraints: Must remain legible against varying watercolor backgrounds
CTA must have a distinct 'magical' glow
designPlan.rationale: A floating organic nav maintains the immersive atmosphere while keeping conversion paths accessible.

layoutSections.contentHints: Studio logo, social icons (nature-themed), copyright, privacy policy, newsletter signup.

designPlan.goal: Close the experience with a sense of studio heritage and a way to stay connected.
designPlan.roleFit: Captures visitors who aren't ready to buy tickets but want to follow the project.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Final success state for the 'joins the mailing list' outcome.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Supports lead generation through a thematic newsletter signup.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Three-column layout: Studio info, Links, Newsletter.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Wide, grounded footer with a hand-painted top border (e.g., rolling hills).
designPlan.visualIntent: Deep forest greens or sunset oranges to provide a 'grounding' contrast to the airy site.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Simple, clean interactions for form fields and social icons.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Studio-first branding to build trust and prestige.
constraints: Newsletter form must be accessible and clearly validated
Social icons should use custom nature-themed variants
designPlan.rationale: The footer acts as a thematic 'end of the journey' that transitions the user back to the studio's broader world.

pages.title: Home
pages.description: The main promotional gateway for the film.

sections.contentHints: Full-screen watercolor landscape video, floating title logo, 'Watch Trailer' play button.

designPlan.role: Page opener and value proposition anchor
designPlan.goal: Transport the user into the film's world within seconds.
designPlan.roleFit: Immediately satisfies the visitor's goal to 'Experience the film's atmosphere'.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: The 'View immersive hero animation' and 'Watch trailer' steps.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Demonstrates the 'Immersive Visual Experience' capability through high-fidelity video.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Centered title and primary action over a full-bleed background.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Centered editorial layout with minimal UI clutter.
designPlan.visualIntent: Cinematic, using a 'living painting' video background with soft ambient motion.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Large, inviting play button that opens a modal trailer.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Lead with the film's title and a poetic tagline.
constraints: Video must be muted and loop seamlessly
Title must be legible over moving video
designPlan.rationale: A centered hero with ambient video is the most effective way to establish the Ghibli-esque 'sense of place'.

sections.intent: Introduce the core conflict and heart of the story
sections.contentHints: Elegant typography over a soft-focus background image, brief poetic summary.

designPlan.goal: Provide context and emotional depth to the visuals seen in the hero.
designPlan.roleFit: Helps the visitor understand the 'why' behind the film.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Supports the 'Read about the world' step.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Uses 'Visual Storytelling' through typography and atmospheric background art.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Single column of centered text with ample whitespace.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Editorial style with large, readable serif fonts.
designPlan.visualIntent: Soft, parchment-like background with watercolor stains at the edges.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Subtle scroll-triggered fade-ins for text blocks.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Poetic, evocative copy rather than a dry plot summary.
constraints: Maintain high contrast for readability on textured backgrounds
designPlan.rationale: An editorial layout focuses the user's attention on the story's emotional core.

sections.contentHints: Horizontal scroll of hand-drawn character portraits with short personality descriptions.

designPlan.goal: Introduce the cast and make the audience care about their journey.
designPlan.roleFit: Satisfies the visitor's core action to 'Explore characters'.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: The 'Read about characters' step of the discovery loop.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Showcases character design as part of the visual storytelling.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: A horizontal row of cards with image, name, and short bio.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Side-scrolling carousel to encourage exploration without taking up vertical space.
designPlan.visualIntent: Cards styled like hand-painted sketches or polaroids.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Smooth drag/swipe interaction with a subtle hover zoom on portraits.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Focus on character traits and their role in the world.
constraints: Must be touch-friendly for mobile users
Images must have alt text describing the character
designPlan.rationale: A carousel allows for a deep dive into multiple characters while maintaining the page's flow.

sections.contentHints: Beautiful sunset background, 'Find a Theater' search bar or button, release date text.

designPlan.goal: Convert emotional interest into a concrete action (booking a ticket).
designPlan.roleFit: Directly serves the visitor's goal to 'Find where to watch'.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: The final 'Check local showtimes' and 'Success' state.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Primary implementation of the 'Ticketing & Lead Gen' capability.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Bold headline, release date, and a prominent search/button group.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Full-width banner with a high-contrast visual anchor.
designPlan.visualIntent: Warm sunset palette to evoke a sense of 'coming home' or 'the end of a journey'.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Clear, high-feedback button states and a simple input for theater search.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Urgency and excitement: 'In Theaters This Summer'.
constraints: CTA must be the most visually prominent element on the page
Search bar must be functional or clearly labeled as a placeholder
designPlan.rationale: The sunset visual provides a natural emotional climax that pairs perfectly with the final call to action.

pageDesignPlan.pageGoal: The main promotional gateway for the film.
pageDesignPlan.roleFit: This page primarily serves Potential Viewer.
pageDesignPlan.capabilityFocus: This page should make Immersive Visual Experience, Ticketing & Lead Gen understandable and actionable.
pageDesignPlan.taskLoopCoverage: Help users progress through Discovery to Conversion, Discovery to Conversion, Discovery to Conversion, Discovery to Conversion.
pageDesignPlan.narrativeArc: Move from orientation to persuasion, then social proof, and finish with a decisive conversion close.
pageDesignPlan.layoutStrategy: Use section rhythm, spacing, and contrast to support the entry stage of the user journey rather than making every block equally loud.
hierarchy: The first viewport should establish context and value quickly.
Mid-page sections should deepen trust or explain the offer.
Lower-page sections should reduce friction and reinforce action.
pageDesignPlan.transitionStrategy: Make transitions feel intentional through spacing, contrast shifts, and restrained motion rather than decorative separators.
sharedShellNotes: Assume global layout sections frame the page experience.
Keep page-specific sections visually coherent with the shared shell.
constraints: Preserve the given section order unless a wrapper or grouping is required for coherence.
Avoid repetitive section pacing; vary density and emphasis across the page.
pageDesignPlan.rationale: Default page planner framed Home around role needs and the MVP journey rather than isolated sections.

pages.title: The World
pages.description: Deep dive into the environmental art and lore.

sections.contentHints: Grid of concept art and movie stills with light-box expansion.

designPlan.goal: Display the film's art in a way that feels like a physical art gallery.
designPlan.roleFit: Satisfies the visitor's goal to see more of the film's unique style.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Deepens the 'Read about the world' step.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Masonry or clean square grid with light-box functionality.
designPlan.visualIntent: Clean, white or off-white background to make the watercolor colors pop.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Click-to-expand lightbox with smooth transitions and caption support.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Let the images lead; use minimal captions for lore context.
designPlan.rationale: A gallery grid is the standard and most effective way to present a collection of high-quality visual assets.

pageDesignPlan.pageGoal: Deep dive into the environmental art and lore.
pageDesignPlan.roleFit: This page primarily serves Potential Viewer.
pageDesignPlan.capabilityFocus: This page should make Immersive Visual Experience understandable and actionable.
pageDesignPlan.taskLoopCoverage: Help users progress through Discovery to Conversion.
pageDesignPlan.narrativeArc: Keep the page tightly focused: establish value, support it, then convert.
pageDesignPlan.layoutStrategy: Use section rhythm, spacing, and contrast to support the evaluation stage of the user journey rather than making every block equally loud.
hierarchy: The first viewport should establish context and value quickly.
Mid-page sections should deepen trust or explain the offer.
Lower-page sections should reduce friction and reinforce action.
pageDesignPlan.transitionStrategy: Make transitions feel intentional through spacing, contrast shifts, and restrained motion rather than decorative separators.
sharedShellNotes: Assume global layout sections frame the page experience.
Keep page-specific sections visually coherent with the shared shell.
constraints: Preserve the given section order unless a wrapper or grouping is required for coherence.
Avoid repetitive section pacing; vary density and emphasis across the page.
pageDesignPlan.rationale: Default page planner framed The World around role needs and the MVP journey rather than isolated sections.
